title: Hermetic coverage, live e2e, and the template mirror
purpose: Prove the family and ship it: the hermetic in-process suite covers every v2 stage outcome with fake-CLI shims emitting captured real norn envelopes, a hermetic dispatch test exercises a two-brief wave with a dependency edge and both halt_on_failure behaviours, the live e2e extends to the dispatch arc against real binaries — including a kill -9 of the server mid-wave to prove durable resume (S3) — and the dev-pipeline template mirrors every family change so `aion new` scaffolds the v2 pipeline. The cluster lands only behind the full gate set (C38). ADR-002 governs the mirror (the template is replaced in place, no parallel template) and ADR-003 governs every test input (no default timeouts anywhere — each scenario states its caps and deadlines).
task: Four fronts, tests and packaging only — no production module under examples/stacked-dev/src/ changes in this brief; a defect exposed here is filed against the owning brief, not patched here. (1) Shims: extend test/support/shims.gleam so the norn shim answers scout and review invocations with captured real completion envelopes ({"output": <report>, ...} as recorded live 2026-06-13 — never invented shapes, P4) and offers per-scenario report variants (scout failure, dev blocked, review drift unfixed, review with fixes, harden regression). (2) Scenarios: extend test/aion_stacked_dev_test.gleam with the seven pipeline outcomes of C33 plus the two-brief dispatch wave of C35 — both live in this one module because the cluster structure pins a single suite module and the exclusive-PATH harness is VM-global under gleeunit's sequential runner (CN9); argv logs prove the '<branch>-scout' / '<branch>' / '<branch>-review' session discipline and that diagnostics and review notes travel intact (C34). (3) Live e2e: extend crates/aion-cli/tests/stacked_dev_live_e2e.rs to deploy all four archives, dispatch a one-brief wave, kill -9 the server while the run is parked in review and reboot it (S3 — scope-forward beyond the checklist text, justified: S3 is assigned here and durable resume is this family's reason to exist), drive the review signal, and assert the enriched brief in the merged result. (4) Template: mirror every family change into crates/aion-cli/templates/dev_pipeline/ with {{name}} placeholders, replacing the old inner-dev shape rather than adding alongside (ADR-002), with the rendered worker's gates green. The final R# states the landing gate set (C38) — note the worker crate is NOT a workspace member and is gated explicitly. Out of scope: any change to workflow/activity/codec/type/schema sources in examples/stacked-dev/src/ or schemas/ (BD-001..BD-006 own those), the design-system canon, and check-schema-drift.sh itself (BD-001 ships it; this brief only runs it).

requirements.title: Norn stage shims with captured real envelopes
requirements.spec: Extend test/support/shims.gleam: the norn shim SHALL distinguish scout, dev, resume, and review invocations by their argv (the scout and review session ids end "-scout" and "-review"; dev uses --session-id with the bare branch; resume uses --resume) and SHALL answer scout and review invocations with the full captured real norn completion envelope — the schema-constrained report under "output" alongside the usage/model/session_id/events keys recorded live 2026-06-13 — exercising the envelope arm of the two-shape parser; the dev/resume replies stay bare DevResult shapes, exercising the bare arm. Provide per-scenario writers: a passing scout report, a scout failure (non-zero exit with stderr), a dev report with a blocked requirement, review reports with alignment "drifted" (empty fixes), with non-empty fixes and all "fixed", and a harden-regression pairing (non-empty fixes while the yg shim fails the post-harden scoped check). Every reply report SHALL validate against its stage contract schema. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT invent envelope shapes or reuse the dev envelope for scout/review (P4), and the shim skeleton (argv logging to <root>/norn.log, exclusive PATH) stays as-is.
acceptance: The scout and review shim replies are JSON objects with an "output" key whose value is the stage report, plus the captured envelope's sibling keys — not a bare report.
Each shim writer's canned report validates against the corresponding package stage schema (scout_report.json / dev_report.json / review_report.json) when checked in a test.
The shim distinguishes the three norn sessions purely from argv, with no environment-variable side channel: a "-scout" session id selects the scout reply, "-review" the review reply.
Existing shim helpers (write_meridian, write_git, write_yg_*) are extended, not forked — one shim harness serves the whole suite.

requirements.title: Hermetic scenarios: every stage outcome and the dispatch wave
requirements.spec: Extend test/aion_stacked_dev_test.gleam. Pipeline scenarios (each stating every cap, backoff, and deadline explicitly — ADR-003, CN2): (a) scout failure fails the run with the typed error naming the scout stage; (b) a dev report with a blocked requirement fails the run with the typed dev-block error; (c) verify exhaustion surfaces rounds and diagnostics (existing scenario, kept green against brief_dev); (d) a review report leaving any requirement "drifted" with empty fixes fails the run with the typed error listing the drifted R# ids and their issues; (e) a review report with non-empty fixes triggers a scoped_checks re-run and a passing re-verify converges the run; (f) a harden regression — non-empty fixes with the post-harden scoped check failing — fails the run with the typed error naming the review stage; (g) the full happy path lands, and the worktree brief file under <root>/.yggdrasil-worktrees/<branch>/docs/design/.../briefs/ carries a scout, dev, and review block on every requirement plus an execution block whose gate and attestation are distinct objects with status "landed", while its authored subset is byte-identical to the fixture brief (the hermetic proxy for "on the landed branch": git and yg are shims, so the worktree file the land step committed is the observable). Session discipline (C34): the norn argv log SHALL show "-scout", bare-branch, and "-review" session ids in that order, scoped-check diagnostics SHALL appear verbatim in the resumed dev argv, and a distinguishable review-note string SHALL appear verbatim in the post-review feedback argv. S7: after the drifted-review failure (d), the worktree brief file still carries the scout and dev blocks written before the failure — a failed run's enrichment-so-far is preserved and post-mortemable. Dispatch scenarios (C35): against a fixture design_dir under the shim root holding two schema-valid briefs with a depends_on edge, (h) a wave listing them dependency-last still runs the dependency's child first (observable by branch order in the yg argv log) and returns two landed outcomes in assembled order; (i) with the first child forced to fail and halt_on_failure true, the result is [failed, skipped] and the yg log shows no provision for the second; (j) the same wave with halt_on_failure false runs both and returns [failed, landed]. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT mark any scenario ignored or gate it behind an env var, and SHALL NOT move the suite off gleeunit's sequential runner (CN9).
acceptance: Scenarios (a)–(g) each exist as a test function and `cd examples/stacked-dev && gleam test` passes with all of them included.

The happy-path test reads the worktree brief file and asserts: scout/dev/review blocks present on every requirement, execution.gate and execution.attestation distinct objects, execution.status == "landed", and the authored-subset encoding byte-identical to the fixture's.
The drifted-review test asserts the typed error carries the drifted R# id and an issue string emitted by the review shim, verbatim.

The session-discipline assertions check the norn log for "--session-id" values ending "-scout" and "-review" and a bare-branch dev session, and the harden-regression test asserts the run fails with the review stage named.
After scenario (d), the test asserts the worktree brief file contains the scout and dev blocks and no review block — the failure preserved everything written before it.

Dispatch tests (h), (i), (j) assert assembled ordering, the exact outcome sequences [landed, landed], [failed, skipped], and [failed, landed], and that halt suppresses the second child's provisioning.

requirements.title: Live e2e: the dispatch arc with a kill -9 resume
requirements.spec: Extend crates/aion-cli/tests/stacked_dev_live_e2e.rs (keeping the existing ChildGuard/flock/port-reservation harness). THE TEST SHALL build and deploy all four family archives (stacked-dev, brief-dev, gate, dispatch — CN6: deployed as a unit), extend the shim set with the R1 scout/review envelope replies and a fixture design_dir containing one schema-valid brief, start a dispatch run with a one-brief wave (every cap and deadline stated in the input JSON; review_deadline_ms sized in days — the engine imposes no bound of its own, ADR-003), and wait for the child to park in_review. THEN it SHALL kill the server with SIGKILL (kill -9), reboot `aion server` from the same config and store, wait for liveness, and confirm via describe that the run is still Running — and that recovery did not re-execute completed activities, observable as exactly one "branch add" line in the yg shim log across the whole test (S3). THEN it SHALL drive the review_verdict approve signal, wait for completion, and assert: the dispatch result in the recorded history carries one landed outcome with the branch and merged_into, and the enriched brief file exists in the merged result — the worktree brief file carries scout/dev/review blocks and the execution block (the shims intercept git/yg, so the committed worktree file is the merged result's observable). THE TEST SHALL NOT use SIGTERM for the mid-run kill (graceful drain would not prove crash durability) and SHALL NOT lower any deadline to force timing.
acceptance: The test deploys four archives and the deploy assertions name dispatch.aion with workflow type "dispatch".
A SIGKILL is delivered to the server process mid-run (after in_review, before the signal), the server is rebooted from the same aion.db, and a subsequent describe reports status Running.
After completion, the yg shim log contains exactly one "branch add" invocation — provisioning was not re-executed across the crash.
The completed history contains a landed outcome for the wave's brief id, and the test reads the worktree brief file asserting the execution block is present with status "landed".
The final shutdown remains the existing graceful SIGTERM path asserting exit code 0.

requirements.title: Mirror the family into the dev-pipeline template
requirements.spec: Mirror every family change into crates/aion-cli/templates/dev_pipeline/ with {{name}} placeholders, replacing in place (ADR-002 — no parallel template, no leftover onatopp-shaped code): project_dev.gleam becomes the v2 inner pipeline ({{name}}_dev keeps its entry-module slot, mirroring brief_dev); project_dispatch.gleam, support/prompts.gleam, and support/enrich.gleam are created mirroring dispatch.gleam, prompts.gleam, and enrich.gleam; support/types.gleam, activities.gleam, locals.gleam, codecs_core.gleam, codecs_flow.gleam, codecs_workflows.gleam, and errors.gleam mirror their example counterparts; schemas/ gains the stage-contract copies (scout_report.json, dev_report.json, review_report.json — content identical to the example's drift-gated copies) and dispatch_input.json/dispatch_output.json, with input.json, dev_input.json, and dev_output.json reshaped; workflow.toml gains the {{name}}_dispatch entry and the updated activities lists; worker/ (handlers.rs, types.rs, main.rs, tests/wire_compat.rs, tests/handlers_shims.rs) mirrors the worker changes; test/project_test.gleam and test/shims.gleam mirror the hermetic suite including the R1 envelope shims; README.md describes the v2 family. Where the example embeds aion-repo specifics (the docs/design-system canon path, check-schema-drift.sh), the template SHALL omit them rather than ship a broken reference — a rendered project has no canon directory to drift against; this is the one deliberate non-mirror, stated in the template README. THE RENDERED OUTPUT SHALL pass its gates: scaffold a project from the template and run cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, and cargo test in its worker, and gleam test in the package.
acceptance: `grep -rn "onatopp" crates/aion-cli/templates/dev_pipeline/` returns nothing, and the template contains project_dispatch.gleam, support/prompts.gleam, and support/enrich.gleam.
The template workflow.toml has four [[workflow]] entries ({{name}}, {{name}}_dev, {{name}}_gate, {{name}}_dispatch) whose activities lists match the example's entries name-for-name.
The template's scout_report.json, dev_report.json, and review_report.json are byte-identical to examples/stacked-dev/schemas/' copies.

Scaffolding a project from the template and running the rendered worker's `cargo fmt --check && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings && cargo test` and the rendered package's `gleam test` all exit 0 (via the repo's existing template-rendering gate or a fresh `aion new` scaffold).
The template README names the deliberate non-mirror (no schema-drift gate in rendered projects) and the four-archive deploy-as-a-unit requirement.

requirements.title: The landing gate set
requirements.spec: Structural, cross-cutting, file-less: the cluster's landing commit passes every gate. From the repo root: cargo fmt --check; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace. The standalone worker crate is NOT a workspace member, so additionally from examples/stacked-dev/worker: cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, and cargo test. From examples/stacked-dev: gleam test, and scripts/check-schema-drift.sh (shipped by BD-001) exits 0. THE LANDING SHALL NOT use #[allow], #[expect], #[ignore], _var renames, or cfg dead-code hiding to satisfy any gate — a firing lint is fixed in the code, and a test needing a runtime resource gates at runtime and reports its skip.
acceptance: `cargo fmt --check` and `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` and `cargo test --workspace` all exit 0 at the landing commit.
`cd examples/stacked-dev/worker && cargo fmt --check && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings && cargo test` exits 0.
`cd examples/stacked-dev && gleam test` exits 0 and `examples/stacked-dev/scripts/check-schema-drift.sh` exits 0.
`grep -rn "#\[ignore\]" examples/stacked-dev/worker crates/aion-cli/tests/stacked_dev_live_e2e.rs` returns nothing introduced by this cluster.

boundaries: THE BRIEF SHALL NOT modify any module under examples/stacked-dev/src/ or any file under examples/stacked-dev/schemas/ — a defect a scenario exposes is reported against the owning brief (BD-001..BD-006), never patched from the test suite.
THE BRIEF SHALL NOT modify the design-system canon under docs/design-system/ or the package drift gate script (BD-001 ships check-schema-drift.sh; this brief only executes it).
THE BRIEF SHALL NOT invent norn envelope or report shapes — every shim reply is the captured real envelope shape or a stage-schema-valid report (P4).
THE BRIEF SHALL NOT move the hermetic suite off gleeunit's sequential runner, parallelise shim-PATH tests, or run e2e suites concurrently with gleam builds in the same checkout (CN9).
THE BRIEF SHALL NOT introduce a default timeout, cap, or deadline anywhere — every scenario and the e2e input state every bound explicitly (ADR-003, CN2).
THE BRIEF SHALL NOT keep a parallel old-shape template or compatibility scaffolding — the dev-pipeline template is replaced in place (ADR-002).
THE BRIEF SHALL NOT add dispatch concurrency scenarios — serial-only is the design's delivery shape until parent-close (RM-001/ADR-004) lands.
verification: Run `cd examples/stacked-dev && gleam test` — every C33 scenario and the three C35 dispatch tests appear in the run output and pass.

Run `grep -n \"output\" examples/stacked-dev/test/support/shims.gleam` and confirm the scout and review shim replies are envelopes with sibling captured keys, while dev/resume replies remain bare — both parser arms exercised.
Run the live e2e (`cargo test -p aion-cli --test stacked_dev_live_e2e`) and confirm in its output the SIGKILL/reboot sequence and the single-provision assertion.
Run `grep -rn "onatopp" crates/aion-cli/templates/dev_pipeline/` — empty; run the template-rendering gate and confirm the rendered worker and package gates pass.
Execute the full R5 gate list at the landing commit and record each exit code — all zero.
Confirm no file under examples/stacked-dev/src/ or examples/stacked-dev/schemas/ changed in this brief's diff.
